patch.value: Extension install self-copy guard: reject source-inside-destination layouts before fs.cp EINVAL (PM-CLI-28)

Sentry PM-CLI-28 records a handled production extension/package install reaching node fs.cp with the destination inside the source tree. Detect source/destination identity and containment before copying, return a deterministic actionable pm error, and cover the real install path without suppressing unrelated filesystem failures. Duplicate check: all-status searches for PM-CLI-28, the exact EINVAL title, and self-copy/subdirectory wording found no canonical item; this is related to package-platform maturity and release telemetry.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm search PM-CLI-28 --status all returned zero; exact title and source/destination containment searches across all statuses found no canonical tracker item. Live Sentry issue PM-CLI-28 count=2, first/last seen 2026-07-13T23:27Z on release 2026.7.13.

value.note: Canonical containment and safe staging for extension copies
Symlink self-copy and root refusal regression coverage

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Implementation evidence: extension copy safety canonicalizes source and destination containment through real paths, safely stages symlink-contained destinations, handles a temp root inside the source, and refuses the filesystem-root edge with extension_install_source_contains_destination. Focused real-filesystem regressions and all strict gates pass at 100/100/100/100 coverage.

author: codex-gpt5

patch.value: Extension install canonicalizes source and destination parents before containment checks; symlinked or temp-root-contained destinations are staged outside the source without fs.cp self-copy; a filesystem-root source fails before copying with extension_install_source_contains_destination; unrelated filesystem errors remain visible; real-filesystem regressions and packed smoke pass.
pm-cli 2026.7.13; Node v26.4.0; Linux; live Sentry PM-CLI-28 plus sandboxed real-filesystem reproduction
Resolve an extension source through a symlink whose canonical destination lies below that source, then install it. Before the fix fs.cp receives a destination inside the source and throws EINVAL.
Install stages the source outside its canonical tree or returns a deterministic pm error before copying when no safe staging parent exists.
The previous copy path compared lexical paths only and reached node fs.cp self-copy EINVAL for canonical containment layouts.
extension and package install copy safety
Extension/package installs could fail with a low-level filesystem exception and create high-severity telemetry noise.

message: Complete issue metadata and reliability graph after implementation proof

patch.value: Canonical realpath containment now stages extension sources safely outside their destination and returns extension_install_source_contains_destination before unsafe root self-copy.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Final verification: pm linked real-filesystem containment suite passes 4/4 with sandbox parity; full coverage and package-first extension dogfood pass; live PM-CLI-28 is resolved.

patch.value: Canonical extension copy containment and safe staging pass real-filesystem regressions, package dogfood, and live Sentry closeout.
